name: vsc-search-everywhere
description: Fast and customizable files, symbols, and content search engine with fuzzy filename matching. Based on vscode-search-everywhere by Kamil Bysiec.

searchAnywhere.shouldInitOnStartup.description: Should indexing be initialized on Visual Studio Code startup.

searchAnywhere.shouldDisplayNotification.description: Should display indexing notification in toast or status bar.

searchAnywhere.shouldHighlightSymbol.description: Should the selected symbol be highlighted.

searchAnywhere.shouldUseDebounce.description: Should the debounce function be used while returning filter results (useful in case of the large workspace).

searchAnywhere.icons.description: Ability to define icons that should be displayed for appropriate item types. According to VSC API, only Octicons are allowed. Not defined item type will not have any icon.

searchAnywhere.itemsFilter.description: Ability to define a filter that should be applied to items.

searchAnywhere.shouldUseItemsFilterPhras.description: Should be a possibility to filter by assigned filter phrases.

searchAnywhere.itemsFilterPhrases.description: Phrases for item type which could be used for narrowing the results down.

searchAnywhere.helpPhrase.description: A phrase that should invoke help.

searchAnywhere.shouldItemsBeSorted.description: Ability to decide whether items should be sorted by type.

searchAnywhere.shouldSearchSelection.description: Ability to decide whether selection in the active editor is put in the search.

searchAnywhere.exclude.description: An array of globs. Any file matching these globs will be excluded from indexing.

searchAnywhere.include.description: String with include pattern. Any file matching this glob will be included in indexing.

searchAnywhere.excludeMode.description: Ability to choose which exclude option should be applied. If gitignore file is not found or is empty, the extension option is used as a fallback. To see the changes from the updated gitignore file after indexing, the reload must be done.

searchAnywhere.shouldWorkspaceDataBeCach.description: Ability to decide if the workspace should be indexed only once. Each next startup of Visual Studio Code will collect data from cache.

searchAnywhere.contentSearch.enabled.description: Enable on-demand content search. When enabled, typing in the search box will also search file contents for matches.

searchAnywhere.contentSearch.exclude.description: An array of globs. Files matching these patterns will be excluded from content search. Content search is not cached to avoid storing large data.

searchAnywhere.contentSearch.maxResults.description: Maximum number of content search results to display.

searchAnywhere.contentSearch.maxFileSize.description: Maximum file size in KB to search content in. Files larger than this will be skipped.
